title: ManufacturingModel Object
description: Represents a ManufacturingModel within a CAM design. A Manufacturing Model is a derive of the Design scene, which can be augmented without any effects of the original Design.

methods.name: activate
methods.description: Makes the ManufacturingModel the active edit target in the user interface. This is the same as enabling the radio button next to the occurrence in the browser.

methods.name: classType
methods.description: Static function that all classes support that returns the type of the class as a string. The returned string matches the string returned by the objectType property. For example if you have a reference to an object and you want to check if it's a SketchLine you can use myObject.objectType == fusion.SketchLine.classType().

methods.name: deleteMe
methods.description: Deletes this ManufacturingModel. If this is part of a setup, the reference to this will be lost. The deletion makes that reference invalid.

methods.name: duplicate
methods.description: Creates and returns a copy of the ManufacturingModel, within its parent collection. The newly created ManufacturingModel will have a new unique name assigned.

methods.name: syncManufacturingModel
methods.description: Checks whether changes to the original design have been made. If so, the given manufacturing model is synchronized with its source.

properties.name: id
properties.description: Gets the unique identifier of the ManufacturingModel within the document.

properties.name: isActive
properties.description: Gets whether this ManufacturingModel is active in the user interface. This is the same as checking the state of the radio button next to the ManufacturingModel in the browser. To activate the ManufacturingModel use the Activate method.

properties.name: isValid
properties.description: Indicates if this object is still valid, i.e. hasn't been deleted or some other action done to invalidate the reference.

properties.name: name
properties.description: Gets or sets the display name of the ManufacturingModel. This is the name that will be shown in the browser and other locations.

properties.name: objectType
properties.description: This property is supported by all objects in the API and returns a string that contains the full name (namespace::objecttype) describing the type of the object.   It's often useful to use this in combination with the classType method to see if an object is a certain type. For example: if obj.objectType == adsk.core.Point3D.classType():

properties.name: occurrence
properties.description: Returns the occurrence for that ManufacturingModel.

related_samples.name: Additive Manufacturing MJF API Sample
related_samples.description:
Demonstrates how to automate the creation of an additive MJF manufacturing setup and arrange components within the build volume of a 3D printer.

To run the sample script, have a design with one or more components open in Fusion’s DESIGN workspace. This script will switch the UI from the DESIGN workspace to the MANUFACTURE workspace, create a new Manufacturing Model, and create an Additive Arrange using that manufacturing model as an input.

The setup will select an MJF 3D printer from Fusion’s machine library and a print setting from the print setting library. All components in the Manufacturing model will be automatically arranged within the build volume of the selected MJF machine.

activate.title: ManufacturingModel.activate Method
activate.description: Makes the ManufacturingModel the active edit target in the user interface. This is the same as enabling the radio button next to the occurrence in the browser.
activate.return_description: Returns true if the activation was successful.

classType.title: ManufacturingModel.classType Method
classType.description: Static function that all classes support that returns the type of the class as a string. The returned string matches the string returned by the objectType property. For example if you have a reference to an object and you want to check if it's a SketchLine you can use myObject.objectType == fusion.SketchLine.classType().
classType.return_description: Returns a string indicating the type of the object.

deleteMe.title: ManufacturingModel.deleteMe Method
deleteMe.description: Deletes this ManufacturingModel. If this is part of a setup, the reference to this will be lost. The deletion makes that reference invalid.
deleteMe.return_description: Returns true if the delete is successful.

duplicate.title: ManufacturingModel.duplicate Method
duplicate.description: Creates and returns a copy of the ManufacturingModel, within its parent collection. The newly created ManufacturingModel will have a new unique name assigned.
duplicate.return_description: Returns the newly created ManufacturingModel copy.

syncManufacturingModel.title: ManufacturingModel.syncManufacturingModel Method
syncManufacturingModel.description: Checks whether changes to the original design have been made. If so, the given manufacturing model is synchronized with its source.
syncManufacturingModel.return_description: Returns true if the manufacturing model needed an update.

id.title: ManufacturingModel.id Property
id.description: Gets the unique identifier of the ManufacturingModel within the document.

syntax.python:
# Get the value of the property.
propertyValue = manufacturingModel_var.id

isActive.title: ManufacturingModel.isActive Property
isActive.description: Gets whether this ManufacturingModel is active in the user interface. This is the same as checking the state of the radio button next to the ManufacturingModel in the browser. To activate the ManufacturingModel use the Activate method.

syntax.python:
# Get the value of the property.
propertyValue = manufacturingModel_var.isActive

isValid.title: ManufacturingModel.isValid Property
isValid.description: Indicates if this object is still valid, i.e. hasn't been deleted or some other action done to invalidate the reference.

syntax.python:
# Get the value of the property.
propertyValue = manufacturingModel_var.isValid

name.title: ManufacturingModel.name Property
name.description: Gets or sets the display name of the ManufacturingModel. This is the name that will be shown in the browser and other locations.

syntax.python:
# Get the value of the property.
propertyValue = manufacturingModel_var.name

# Set the value of the property.
manufacturingModel_var.name = propertyValue

objectType.title: ManufacturingModel.objectType Property
objectType.description: This property is supported by all objects in the API and returns a string that contains the full name (namespace::objecttype) describing the type of the object. It's often useful to use this in combination with the classType method to see if an object is a certain type. For example: if obj.objectType == adsk.core.Point3D.classType():

syntax.python:
# Get the value of the property.
propertyValue = manufacturingModel_var.objectType

occurrence.title: ManufacturingModel.occurrence Property
occurrence.description: Returns the occurrence for that ManufacturingModel.

syntax.python:
# Get the value of the property.
propertyValue = manufacturingModel_var.occurrence

related_samples.name: Additive Manufacturing MJF API Sample
related_samples.description:
Demonstrates how to automate the creation of an additive MJF manufacturing setup and arrange components within the build volume of a 3D printer.

To run the sample script, have a design with one or more components open in Fusion’s DESIGN workspace. This script will switch the UI from the DESIGN workspace to the MANUFACTURE workspace, create a new Manufacturing Model, and create an Additive Arrange using that manufacturing model as an input.

The setup will select an MJF 3D printer from Fusion’s machine library and a print setting from the print setting library. All components in the Manufacturing model will be automatically arranged within the build volume of the selected MJF machine.
